title: Developing a competency framework for effective assistive technology training
body: "This qualitative research was designed to examine and identify the skills and knowledge required by staff in special schools and colleges to:   assess the digital assistive technology (AT) needs of pupils support pupils to use digital AT in the classroom   The project involved 36 staff from special schools and colleges and included focus groups, interviews and a literature review.  The main output is a framework that maps the skills and knowledge needed by special school and college staff working in student-facing roles across differing staff roles. It is detailed in the report and the accompanying ‘Assistive technology in special education contexts: competency framework’.  Effective multi-agency working with assistive technology reports on findings from research exploring how different agencies collaborate in providing and implementing assistive technology.  "

taxons.title: Special educational needs and disability (SEND) and high needs
